title: Pleasing Crisp Spanish Vegetable Cocido Madrileno
description: A malty, crisp, and energizing Spanish dish featuring vegan white wine vinegar and lacquer glaze, this vegetarian friendly cocido madrileno is perfect for a hearty lunch. It combines a variety of vegetables and beans in a flavorful broth.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes, cooking for 5 minutes.
Add the chickpeas, vegetable broth, white wine vinegar, and smoked paprika, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir in the lacquer glaze and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the cocido madrileno hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro.
Let the dish cool completely before refrigerating or freezing.
Reheat the cocido madrileno over low heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through.
